# Shed Industries

> Shed Industries is a UK brand strategy consultancy helping growing businesses get clear on their brand, their market, and their path forward. Founded by Mark McAulay, who brings twenty years of design agency experience and six years as a founder building a brand in the food and drink sector from the ground up.

Shed Industries works with businesses on four things that determine whether a brand competes or gets left behind: market research, competitor analysis, messaging, and brand roadmap. The consultancy is built for growing businesses whose brand hasn't kept pace with their growth — the moment worked at an earlier stage but is losing its edge in a larger, sharper market.

## Services

- [Market Research](https://shed.io/work/market-research/): Evidence-based research into who the customers are, what they value, and how their expectations are shifting.
- [Competitor Analysis](https://shed.io/work/competitor-analysis/): Mapping how competitors are positioned, where they are vulnerable, and what space is available to own.
- [Messaging](https://shed.io/work/messaging/): Defining what a business says, how it says it, and to whom — the narrative a brand carries into every market and conversation.
- [Brand Roadmap](https://shed.io/work/brand-roadmap/): A clear, prioritised plan for what changes, what stays, what gets built, and in what order.
- [Work overview](https://shed.io/work/): All four services and how they build on each other.

## Company

- [About](https://shed.io/about/): Background on Shed Industries and founder Mark McAulay.
- [Contact](https://shed.io/contact/): Get in touch to discuss brand strategy.

## Journal

Honest thinking on brand positioning, market dynamics, and messaging, published when it's worth reading.

- [Journal index](https://shed.io/journal/): All articles.
- [The Blast-Beat Guide to Brand Strategy](https://shed.io/journal/the-blast-beat-guide-to-brand-strategy/): What Napalm Death and Dolly Parton have in common, and what it means for building a brand that lasts.
- [When to Think About Brand Strategy](https://shed.io/journal/when-to-think-about-brand-strategy/): The right moment for brand strategy is rarely forced.
- [The Comfortable Danger Zone](https://shed.io/journal/the-comfortable-danger-zone/): What settled success can hide, and how to see vulnerability before it becomes a problem.
- [What a Brand Roadmap Actually Is](https://shed.io/journal/what-a-brand-roadmap-actually-is/): Why a roadmap is a prioritised plan, not a vision statement.
